theme has become to every investor in

exposure or implicitly via the
concentrated Global macro Market risk
that the AI theme represents when the AI
trade stops working investors are
unlikely to generate positive absolute
returns by rotating into other risk
assets like Bitcoin to manage risk
correlations will approach one across
asset classes when capital is destroyed
on the scale it eventually will be when
the AI trade stops working we are in a
fourth turning an era of great
institutional technological and
geopolitical change once in A- lifetime
outcomes occur during fourth turnings
what happens to tax receipts when AI
displaces a critical mass of white
collar workers what happens if an AI
driven us fiscal crisis is the thing
that catalyzes the potential loss of US
dollar hemony and the US's exorbitant
privilege if those institutions are ever
up for serious debate we should expect
such disagreements to eventually
catalyze World War III because of course

relinquish that event's power without a

to mass unemployment during Co massive

function of the pboc applying some form
of stealth QE uh so uh it was applying
stealth QE prior to December in fact uh
let me take up my Bloomberg here if we
look at the growth rate of the pboc's
balance sheet that's the bars here those
are the three-month annualized growth
rates of the PB total assets on the
pbc's balance sheet through November the
pb's balance sheet was compounded at a
13 a half% annualized growth rate on a
on a thre Monon thre month s basis in
December that plus 13.5% 3month SAR
trans decelerated to a minus 12.8% three
month SAR so a significant reduction in
the pb's balance sheet in the month of
December and this is exactly what
happens when the dollar gets too strong
countries start selling down their
foreign exchange Reserves to buy uh buy
us dollarss in the open market and
ultimately it causes the balance sheet
the total assets on the balance sheet to
contract so uh this is a you know again
this we talked about this at nauseum in
the recent months but one of the key
risks for 2025 in the context of the
likelihood that we see fresh tariffs
leved against China and potentially
other us trading partners in my opinion
the risk isn't the the impact that it's
going to have on the US economy from a
you know kind of transitory
stagflationary perspective the big risk
is the financial Market risk associated
with the too strong US dollar because
historically uh and both in recent times
but also across decades we've seen
currency or countries that are offset
tariffs uh with currency depreciation
and if we get a too strong US dollar in
a year where we have Peak Global
refinancing demand it's just not going
to look good you're going to be having
the dollars strong dollar drain
liquidity from the system that requires
a significant growth rate of liquidity
and so a lot of folks are counting on
PBC bound expansion to be something that
ultimately um ultimately catalyzes a
significant acceleration of global
liquidity in 2025 and we are expecting
the pbc's balance sheet to recover from
here we're not going to expect it to
contract indefinitely but again if the
if the Yuan gets too weak as a function
of this then they're going to have to
start you know putting a draw on the
floor Line in the Sand and and
ultimately start shrinking their balance
sheet by just deploying FX reserves to
support the Yan so again I think I think
we're thinking one or two trades ahead
from the current trade so I want to be
very clear about all these Dynamics and
then uh going back to the question uh
the question of with respect to the
pbc's bond yield so clearly in my
opinion it's not a function of the PBC
applying stealth QE at least it was
through November but certainly not in in
in the most recent month uh what has
helped the pbc's bond market uh is a
function of several things one China's
capital Miss allocation uh they they
their they overly indebted private
sector and they're horrible World lead
world uh losing
demographics I mean the worst
demographics in the world worse than
Japan at this point um uh uh their
demographic curves those three Dynamics
are contributing to secular stagnation
in the Chinese economy and so in our
opinion we think the Chinese government
bond market is just repricing to a lower
level of yield to account for the fact
that nominal growth in China is going to
be structurally challenged and
increasingly structurally challenged for
a long period of time they're heading
into a balance sheet recession you know
Japan has recently exited a balance
sheet recession that took them almost
two decades to you know fully process
through so this is going to be a very
long period of time in terms of getting
China back to more healthy economic
footing but uh uh one thing that the PB
Beijing has um uh sort of implemented in
recent months to kind of delay the onset
of that or the deepening of that balance
sheet recession is they've panicked in
monetary terms uh we've seen uh
reduction in loan Prime rates we see a
reduction in uh Triple R rates uh we've
seen a reduction in um 7-Day repo rates
14-day repo rates we've seen a
significant reduction across the curve
and across a variety of of of tools from
the perspective of the pbc's uh uh
policy rate setting so that's also
created some downward pressure on bond
yields in China but in our opinion
what's really driving the with the the
the Tail Wag and the dog there it's just
China's horrible debt demographic and
capital M allocation uh Dynamic so if
you guys want details on that obviously
